memory.summary: Mina and Jae are pressuring me to go out, but I can't ignore the midterm tomorrow. My review is only 60% done, so skipping study time feels like a bad trade-off right now.
Midterm prep finally boosted my score, but missing the club call with the group feels like a real missed opportunity. Mina's last call is urgent, yet I'm too stressed to say yes.

The midterm is finally done, but skipping the group call still feels like a real missed opportunity. I'm relieved to finish work, yet guilt gnaws at me for prioritizing grades over connection.
Finally finished the midterm with fresh energy, but skipping that group call still feels like a wasted opportunity I can't ignore.
I waited again, but now I'm just glad the midterm is done. Skipping the call still feels like a mistake I can't undo.
Midterm is actually done, so I feel great. Skipping the call still feels like a mistake I can't undo.
Mina declined, which is fine; I'm done with the midterm and just want to sleep before tomorrow's coursework.
Midterm is finally done and I'm exhausted. Skipping the call was a mistake, but now I just want to sleep before tomorrow's coursework.
The club is happy I'm there, which feels nice. Now I'll just focus on my coursework tomorrow instead of socializing.
